page: Microsoft Azure FX32-8ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family, designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The server is equipped with 672.0 GB of total memory, yielding 84.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score of 2369.0 and single-threaded gzip compression at 34.8 GB/sec both ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 8881.34 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8692.0, both falling into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 691.25 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 51.23 GB/sec. For database operations, the server achieves 13.15 million Redis SET operations per second and a PassMark database score of 6030.9.

The primary tradeoff of the FX32-8ms_v2 is its extremely high memory-to-vCPU ratio, which provides massive memory capacity but limits parallel processing power due to having only 8 vCPUs. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets where CPU core licensing or compute requirements are secondary to RAM capacity. Conversely, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded tasks, such as medium-sized LLM text generation (7.33 tokens/sec). This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching layers, and large-scale text processing applications.
description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the FXmsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. The server is configured with 672.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 84.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2369.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 34.8 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance remains average, as seen in its multi-core Geekbench score of 8692.0. This resource profile makes the server highly efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute, such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and text processing pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: An ultra-high memory-to-vCPU ratio server designed for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and single-threaded performance efficiency.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
672.0 GB of total RAM providing 84.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression benchmarks
No local storage, relying on external Premium Storage capabilities
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing
